text.title: caching-write-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for caching-write-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using write-behind for user-facing entities (profiles, orders) because "Redis is fast".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A crash between cache write and DB flush means a user's update disappears silently. Write-behind is only safe when the data-loss window is explicitly budgeted (analytics, counters) and a crash-recovery path exists.
